acceptance_targets: [CH06_CRAFT_KAI_CONCESSION] Across the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:171 through snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:195, Kai's final choice must follow a new, explicit on-page reason beyond Lena simply wanting to keep the card. Pass if a cold reader can state why he lowers the button in character-specific terms; fail if the scene still reduces to 'he knows it is dangerous, calls it dangerous, then allows it anyway because she asked.'

[CH06_CRAFT_MARGOT_OVERSTATEMENT] In revised text from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:269 through snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:291, the scene should have one clear arrival point where Margot understands what disclosure would cost, and every sentence after that point must either add a new consequence or convert the realization into action. Fail if the draft still explains Petra/log exposure twice before she puts the phone away.
[R001] Ch06's first reference to Petra contains no description of her hair color or grey hair.
[R004] Ch06's medical bay entry does not open with an olfactory inventory of the space.
[R018] Ch06 contains no more than 6 instances of the word 'corridor.'

findings.acceptance_test: Across the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:171 through snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:195, Kai's final choice must follow a new, explicit on-page reason beyond Lena simply wanting to keep the card. Pass if a cold reader can state why he lowers the button in character-specific terms; fail if the scene still reduces to 'he knows it is dangerous, calls it dangerous, then allows it anyway because she asked.'
findings.problem: Kai backs off the call button too quickly. The scene asks the reader to believe that an athlete who has just been catastrophically injured by the same system, and who already asked for staff presence in the prior chapter, will let Lena suppress a live missing-time episode after one thin argument about ownership. The chapter wants this beat to create bond, but the concession currently reads partly like scene necessity.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the choice sequence around snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:171, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:173, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:181, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:187, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:193 so Kai's backing down follows a visible pressure change. Options include giving him a stronger, character-true reason to respect Lena's autonomy here, making Lena offer a concrete safeguard he can accept, or having him hold the line longer before a new fact or emotional recognition shifts him.

findings.acceptance_test: In revised text from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:269 through snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:291, the scene should have one clear arrival point where Margot understands what disclosure would cost, and every sentence after that point must either add a new consequence or convert the realization into action. Fail if the draft still explains Petra/log exposure twice before she puts the phone away.
findings.problem: Margot's final scene reaches its decisive insight by the first disclosure inventory: reporting the feed vector would expose her overrides, her appetite, and the kind of coach she is, and what she recoils from is loss rather than guilt. The later Petra/log paragraph then re-proves the same point instead of escalating it, so the chapter's closing stroke lands softer than it should.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten or re-sequence the ending around snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:269, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:273, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:275, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:281, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_06.md:285. Once the chapter has made clear that disclosure would expose the logs and cost Margot the feed, move directly into a new pressure or into the physical act of not calling. Do not spend a second paragraph restating Petra's thoroughness and the same incriminating evidence unless that paragraph changes the stakes.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch06's first reference to Petra contains no description of her hair color or grey hair.
findings.evidence: Ch05 line 1239: 'grey hair pulled back too tight, fatigue written across her face in old familiar lines'; Ch06 line 1422: 'The grey in her hair looked harsher under the strip lights. The clinical precision of her posture had a new rigidity to it'
findings.problem: Petra Engel's grey hair is described in ch05 (first full physical description at line 1239) and re-described in the very next chapter at line 1422. Adjacent-chapter re-introduction of the same physical trait.
findings.rewrite_direction: In ch06, cut the grey-hair descriptor at line 1422. Petra can be identified by posture or action alone; the reader met her hair one chapter ago. Keep the rigidity detail if needed but drop 'The grey in her hair looked harsher under the strip lights.'

findings.acceptance_test: Ch06's medical bay entry does not open with an olfactory inventory of the space.
findings.evidence: Ch05 line 1069: 'smelled like isopropyl and cold metal trays'; Ch06 line 1500: 'The medical bay smelled of antiseptic and the faint mineral trace of surgical polymer, graft work, maintenance fluid'
findings.problem: The medical bay is entered via smell inventory in ch05 (line 1069, Kai's POV) and re-entered via a parallel smell inventory in the very next chapter (ch06, line 1500, Lena's POV). Both open with 'The medical bay smelled [like/of]' + clinical odor list. Adjacent-chapter scene-entry template reuse.
findings.rewrite_direction: In ch06, cut or rework the smell-led entry at line 1500. Enter the medical bay through what has changed since ch05 -- the corridor overflow, the waiting athletes, the symptom forms -- rather than re-inventorying the room's baseline scent. The smell was established one chapter ago.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch06 contains no more than 6 instances of the word 'corridor.'
findings.evidence: 'corridor' appears 10 times in ch06 (lines 1416-1708). Manuscript total: 59. Ch06 accounts for 17% of all corridor mentions.
findings.problem: Ch06 uses 'corridor' 10 times in approximately 290 lines. The chapter's crisis scenario (athletes queuing, overflow into corridors) motivates several uses, but the word itself has become a tic. Manuscript target for a 59-occurrence image is 8-10 retained instances total.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace 4-5 of the 10 'corridor' instances in ch06 with spatial synonyms (hallway, passage) or implied movement. Keep 'corridor' where it carries specific institutional atmosphere.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
